concerns.text: Task T022 implements a conditional fallback to 'Repeated 5-Fold CV' if N < 100, which silently drops the 'hold-out test set' requirement mandated by FR-004 and US-2. The spec explicitly requires evaluation on a hold-out set (or a specific fallback in Edge Cases for <50 records), but T022 hardcodes a threshold of 100 and removes the hold-out artifact entirely for 50 <= N < 100, violating the constraint to 'evaluate on a hold-out test set'.

Tasks T025 and T026 conflate the 95% Confidence Interval (CI) source. FR-008 and US-2 require the CI to be 'derived from the cross-validation folds'. T026 introduces a 'Permutation Test' for p-values, but the task descriptions do not explicitly separate the CI calculation (from CV folds) from the permutation logic. This risks implementing the CI from the permutation distribution (bootstrapping) rather than the CV folds, violating the specific source constraint in FR-008.

Task T027 implements an 'F-test comparison' but fails to explicitly link this to the 'statistically significant improvement over a null model' requirement in SC-002. SC-002 requires a p < 0.05 improvement over a null model, while SC-003 requires comparison against a Linear Regression baseline. T027's description is ambiguous about which comparison satisfies SC-002, risking a silent relaxation of the statistical rigor required by the spec.

Task T014 implements a 'halt if filtered retention < 95%' check. While this aligns with SC-005, the task description does not explicitly state that this check applies to the *filtered* input records (post-family filtering) as required by SC-005. If the task applies this to the raw download, it violates the constraint. The task must explicitly reference 'filtered input records' to preserve the SC-005 constraint.

Task T034 implements classification logic (Success/Inconclusive/Failure) but omits the 'p < 0.05' condition required for 'Success' in the Plan and Constitution (Principle VII). The spec (US-3) only mentions R² thresholds, but the Plan and Constitution explicitly add the p-value constraint for 'Success'. T034 must include the p-value check to satisfy the full constraint set defined in the Plan/Constitution, otherwise it silently relaxes the success criteria.
